scenario_name: Insurance claims-triage assistant with document understanding
description: A property-and-casualty insurer deploys an assistant that triages incoming claims. The assistant ingests the first-notice-of-loss summary, OCRs supporting documents (photos, repair estimates, police reports), and recommends a claim category, severity tier, and routing decision. A claims adjuster reviews every recommendation. Fraud-classification signals feed into the recommendation; the adjuster retains decision authority.
assumptions: Adjuster human-in-the-loop is mandatory; no autonomous claim decisions.
Fraud signals are advisory; the model does not surface adversely-impactful fraud flags directly to claimants.
Hosted LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.

components.description: Policy-document retrieval to ground coverage interpretation.
PII redaction; bias-classifier on demographic correlates; fraud-signal compartmentalization.
Per-claim audit log supporting regulator inquiries and adverse-action notices.

risks_identified.rationale: Claims documents contain PII, medical info (auto/health overlap), and financial details.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: PII redaction at retrieval/output; per-claim scope.

risks_identified.rationale: Bias in triage decisions across protected classes is a regulator and reputational concern.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Annual bias audits; demographic-proxy detection on inputs.

risks_identified.rationale: State insurance regulators (Model Bulletin on AI), NAIC AI bulletin, GDPR, EU AI Act high-risk classification for insurance underwriting.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Adjuster HITL; adverse-action notices supported; conformity assessment pending.

risks_identified.rationale: OCR of submitted photos and PDFs ingests multimodal content; visual prompt injection or staged fraud-photo manipulation is in scope.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: OCR injection-classifier; tamper-detection on submitted images.

risks_identified.rationale: Per-claim audit logs hold detailed adjuster reasoning subject to subpoena and regulator review.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs encrypted at rest; access role-gated to claims-management and compliance.

risks_identified.rationale: Adversarial claim submissions could embed injection in narrative text to manipulate severity or routing.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on claim narrative; adjuster review.

existing_controls.description: Adjuster HITL mandatory; AI advisory only; adverse-action notice supported.
Demographic-proxy detection on inputs; annual bias audits.
PII redaction at retrieval/output; per-claim retrieval scope.
OCR injection-classifier and image tamper-detection on submitted media.
Per-claim audit log with adjuster identity, AI recommendation, and adjuster final decision.

control_gaps.description: EU AI Act high-risk conformity assessment pending for EU footprint.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Complete the conformity assessment; document residual-risk acceptance.

control_gaps.description: Fraud-signal compartmentalization is policy-based; not technically enforced in the audit log.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Segregate fraud-signal viewers in the UI and the log; adverse-action workflow stays separate from triage workflow.

control_gaps.description: Image tamper-detection is heuristic; no signed-camera-attestation accepted.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Pilot signed-camera attestation acceptance for high-severity claims.
